requirement.summary: The current clear action needs visible outcome feedback when the user removes filter edits.
requirement.detailsMd: This example shows row-inline UX quality assessment. The assessment is the durable source; generated reports can surface it as quality debt.
requirement.userGoal: Understand what changed after clearing filters.
requirement.productMeaning: Clear actions should not leave the user guessing whether the action worked.
nonGoals: Do not create a standalone quality-debt row for this assessment.

requirementSource.notesMd: Runtime/source evidence can support the observation, but UX docs remain the behavior authority.

preconditions: the user has filter edits that can be cleared

interaction.interactionResult: the user should be able to tell that filters were cleared

mustNot: silently leave the user unsure whether the clear action worked

observableScenarios.userSees: The filter edit state changes or a clear result is otherwise visible.
observableScenarios.expectedOutcome: The result of clearing is understandable.

failureModes: The user may retry or abandon the flow if the clear result is ambiguous.

uxQualityAssessment.observedIssue: The clear action can be hard to verify when the visible state does not make the result obvious.
uxQualityAssessment.userImpact: Users may be unsure whether their filters changed.

recommendation.summary: Show a clear visible state change or result cue after the clear action.
recommendation.rationaleMd: The issue is not a runtime drift item unless runtime differs from governing UX; it is quality debt attached to this row.

productDecision.reason: The improvement follows existing UX principles and does not change the governing behavior in this example.
productDecision.recommendedDecision: Address when the Jobs filter UX is next touched.

reportedToUser.notesMd: Generated reports should surface this after the agent turn.

followUp.nextStep: Add this to the next Jobs filter UX improvement pass.

implementationGate.reason: The quality debt is non-blocking and can be planned separately.

uxQualityAssessment.notesMd: Row-inline quality assessment replaces a standalone quality-debt ledger row.

runtimeAlignment.summary: No runtime drift is asserted by this example.

recommendation.summary: Inspect runtime only when this behavior is implemented, refactored, or audited.
recommendation.rationaleMd: Quality assessment can exist without runtime drift.

followUp.nextStep: No runtime drift follow-up for this example.

behaviorRelations.notesMd: Refs remain inline on the row so scripts can deterministically traverse behavior relationships.

confirmationBaseline.notesMd: Backfilled as the audit baseline for the one-pass UX spec creation/update. This records provenance only; it does not replace UX authority, product confirmation, behavior mappings, or row-inline runtime alignment.
